feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Fiori
feed.subtitle: SAP Fiori Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Recommended alternatives to Report Painter for multi-step Income Statements in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am looking for the current best practice to replace Report Painter for financial reporting in SAP S/4HANA.

Specifically, I need to generate a multi-step Income Statement (Profit and Loss statement) that displays various profit levels in a specific order, such as:

  1. Revenue

  2. Cost of Sales

  3. Gross Profit (Subtotal 1 - 2)

  4. Selling, General and Administrative Expenses

  5. Operating Profit (Subtotal 3 - 4) ...and so on.

The Challenges:

  • Report Painter/Report Writer is considered maintenance mode (deprecated) in S/4HANA, so we want to move toward a more future-proof solution.

  • The standard Financial Statement Version (FSV) in Fiori/GUI typically starts with "Net Income/Loss" at the top of the hierarchy. Expanding the tree from the top-down does not meet our requirement for a traditional "Report Form" (step-by-step calculation) layout where Revenue comes first.

My Questions:

  • What is the recommended standard tool or architectural approach in S/4HANA to achieve this "Report Form" layout without heavy custom development?

  • Is there a way to define custom subtotals (like Operating Profit) that are not natively defined as a single node in the FSV hierarchy?

  • Should we look into Fiori-based analytical apps, or is an Excel-based integration (like Analysis for Office) the primary direction now?

I would appreciate any insights on the "SAP Standard" way to handle this.

Best regards,

entry.title: Issue with Updating Operation wise Confirmation Time via RAP Application
entry.content:
Hello Expert,

I am currently working on an SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud RAP application where we have implemented a custom field called “Execution Time” as per the business requirement.

The requirement is to update the execution time operation wise in the standard transaction CO03. For this purpose, we are using EML with I_ProductionOrderConfirmationTP to update the fields OpConfirmedWorkQuantity1 and OpConfirmedWorkQuantity2, which are intended to store the time values.

We have written the necessary logic to update both quantity and time using the same EML statement. However, the changes are not getting reflected as expected in the system.

For your reference, we have also attached a snapshot of the Time field along with the relevant code.

Could you please review and suggest if there is an alternative approach or any correction required to achieve the desired update?

Best Regards,

Thank you,

READ ENTITIES OF i_productionordconfirmationtp
ENTITY productionorderconfirmation
EXECUTE getconfproposal
FROM VALUE #(( confirmationgroup = lv_opconf
             %param-confirmationyieldquantity = <lfs_childbillet>-totalweight
            ) )
        RESULT DATA(lt_confproposal)
        REPORTED DATA(lt_reported_conf).

LOOP AT lt_confproposal ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_confproposal>).
APPEND INITIAL LINE TO lt_confirmation ASSIGNING FIELD SYMBOL(<ls_confirmation>).
       <ls_confirmation>-%cid = 'Conf' && sy-tabix.
       <ls_confirmation>-%data = CORRESPONDING #( <ls_confproposal>-%param ).
       <ls_confirmation>-%data-ConfirmedExecutionStartTime = lv_start_ts.
       <ls_confirmation>-%data-ConfirmedExecutionEndTime   = lv_end_ts.
       <ls_confirmation>-%data-finalconfirmationtype = 'X'.

 READ ENTITIES OF i_productionordconfirmationtp
      ENTITY productionorderconfirmation
      EXECUTE getgdsmvtproposal
FROM VALUE #( ( confirmationgroup = <ls_confproposal>-confirmationgroup
            %param-confirmationyieldquantity =  <lfs_childbillet>-totalweight
            ) )
      RESULT DATA(lt_gdsmvtproposal)
      REPORTED DATA(lt_reported_gdsmvt).
      CHECK lt_gdsmvtproposal[] IS NOT INITIAL.
      CLEAR lt_target[].
      DELETE lt_gdsmvtproposal[] WHERE %param-quantityinentryunit IS INITIAL.

        LOOP AT lt_gdsmvtproposal ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_gdsmvtproposal>)
        WHERE confirmationgroup = <ls_confproposal>-confirmationgroup .
        APPEND INITIAL LINE TO lt_target ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_target>).
       
        <ls_target> = CORRESPONDING #( <ls_gdsmvtproposal>-%param ).
        <ls_target>-%cid = 'Item' && sy-tabix.
        ENDLOOP.

  APPEND VALUE #( %cid_ref = <ls_confirmation>-%cid
                  %target = lt_target
  confirmationgroup = <ls_confproposal>-confirmationgroup ) TO lt_matldocitm.

      ENDLOOP.

      MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_productionordconfirmationtp
      ENTITY productionorderconfirmation
      CREATE FROM lt_confirmation
      CREATE BY \_prodnordconfmatldocitm FROM lt_matldocitm
      MAPPED DATA(poconfirmmapped)
      FAILED DATA(poconfirmfailed)
      REPORTED DATA(poconfirmreported).

    ENDIF.

entry.title: Develop custom report in SAP S/4 HANA public cloud
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am currently working with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Version 2602.0.59 and would like to develop a custom report within the system. Could you please guide me on the required steps to achieve this?

Any insights, best practices, or resources on how to approach this task in SAP S/4HANA Cloud would be greatly appreciated.

Looking forward to your responses!

Best regards,
Harshita

entry.title: F3445 Maintain PIRs
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a few issues with Fiori App "F3445 Maintain PIRs".

1. When I download to spreadsheet, all values are being contained in first column A of the Excel and delimited by comma:

But in this blog it shows, that it is downloaded with values in different columns. Is this configurable?:

Creating Independent Requirements from a spreadsheet

2. I do not have button "Version Active":

I need like here:

Thanks.

entry.title: How to set a GroupHeaderListItem inactive in tabular suggestion
entry.content:
Hello community. I am struggling with the grouping function for the tabular suggestion of an Input. The basic grouping works just fine but the created row (GroupHeaderListItem) is always active and selectable which confuses users. I have tryed the 

"groupHeaderFactory" function that is called correctly but the table itself seems to ignore the "type" property of the GroupHeaderListItem. See the code below:
  private createGroupHeader(groupTitle: { key: string }): GroupHeaderListItem {
    return new GroupHeaderListItem({ title: groupTitle.key, type: 'Inactive' });
  }

I am on version 1.139.0.

Is this a bug or is there an other way to get the GroupHeader inactive? Thanks.

/BR Tobias

entry.title: How to create a Segment in Fiori?
entry.content:
Hello Community,

Can anybody help me by outlining the steps to create a segment for segment reporting in Fiori? I am able to accomplish this in GUI, but I also want to be able to do it on our Fiori system.

I would be extra grateful if someone could provide the following information, specifically:

-Necessary roles

-Necessary catalogs/apps

-Step-by-step guide on segment creation

(I know how to select a segment when creating profit center master data; I just need to know how to create a new segment.)

entry.title: Creation and maintenance of multiple app variants of the same app in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Dear community,

Customer is required adaptation and enhancement of standard SAP Fiori App for multiple countries/company codes/locations. As the result more than 20 different variants might be needed for the same app. In addition, target users are not capable to use Adapt UI properly as key-users so all changes are done centrally by development team. They are defining set of standard and custom fields that might be used for all locations and then limit them for each case.

While it is possible to create each variant (but quite difficult). it seems that maintaining it might be even harder - adding new field would require adaptation of all existing variants.

What is the best approach to manage this amount of variants?

What are the possible options to have dynamically defined variant configuration? 

What is the authorisation process to make sure that each location accesses only their app variant? (We need to block access to standard app and other variants)

 

Thank you,

BR Semyon

entry.title: Standard Application Enhancement
entry.content: Are there other ways to enhance standard applications without changing its namespace and id aside from Key User Adaptation? I need to insert my custom code but as a requirement, the personalized views must remain so a new namespace and id for the app is not an option.

entry.title: How to Pass a Filter Parameter to OData V4 TreeTable Hierarchy Batch Calls in Fiori Elements?
entry.content:
I am building a Fiori Elements List Report page with a TreeTable using the sap.fe.templates.ListReport template and OData V4. The table uses SAP Hierarchy annotations (com.sap.vocabularies.Hierarchy.v1) with a hierarchyQualifier.

My manifest configuration looks like this:

"controlConfiguration": {
    "@com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.LineItem": {
        "tableSettings": {
            "type": "TreeTable",
            "hierarchyQualifier": "ResponseHierarchy",
            "selectionMode": "None"
        }
    }
}

The Problem

When the page loads, Fiori Elements automatically fires 3 batch calls:

GET ResponseHierarchy(db05dc96-ea30-4393-a929-266e1c8d2331)
GET ResponseHierarchy/$count
GET ResponseHierarchy?$apply=com.sap.vocabularies.Hierarchy.v1.TopLevels(
    HierarchyNodes=$root/ResponseHierarchy,
    HierarchyQualifier='ResponseHierarchy',
    NodeProperty='ID',
    Levels=1
)&$select=...&$count=true&$skip=0&$top=240

I need to pass a programCycleId to all 3 calls so my backend handler knows which program's data to fetch. The first call works fine because the ID is part of the key segment. However, calls 2 and 3 hit the entity set directly ($root/ResponseHierarchy) without any key or filter.

What I Have Tried

1. Passing ID as route key

{ "pattern": "ResponseHierarchy({ID}):?query:" }

This works for call 1 but calls 2 and 3 don't carry the ID.

2. Passing $filter as query parameter

oParams = { query: { "$filter": "programCycleId eq '" + sProgramId + "'" } };

The $filter in the route URL is not propagated to the $apply=TopLevels(...) and $count calls. The TreeTable control generates these calls referencing $root/ResponseHierarchy directly, completely ignoring the URL $filter.

3. Custom HTTP Header

I ended up using a workaround, setting a custom header x-program-cycle-id on the OData model and reading it in the backend:

oModel.changeHttpHeaders({ "x-program-cycle-id": sProgramCycleId });

This works but it's not ideal because:

  • The header is set globally on the model, affecting all other OData calls
  • It requires manual cache busting (getAllBindings().filter(...).forEach(b => b.refresh()))
  • It's not a clean OData-native solution

My Question

Is there a proper OData V4 / Fiori Elements native way to pass a filter or parameter that gets included in ALL 3 TreeTable hierarchy batch calls, including the $apply=TopLevels(HierarchyNodes=$root/ResponseHierarchy...) call?

Specifically I would like to know:

  1. Is it possible to configure the TreeTable binding via annotations so that a $filter is always appended to all hierarchy calls including the $root/ResponseHierarchy reference inside $apply?

  2. Is there a manifest.json setting or UI5 Fiori Elements API that allows scoping the hierarchy data to a specific filter value without resorting to custom headers?

  3. Is the custom header approach actually the recommended workaround for this scenario, or is there a better pattern?

Any guidance or pointers to documentation would be greatly appreciated!

entry.title: Navigation to Record Inspection Results (F168A) Object Page
entry.content:
I want to navigate to Record Inspection Results (F168A) Object Page with Specific Inspection Lot and MaterialSample from my custom Fiori app. But everytime while navigating all Samples show up in the Object page although I want only the Sample which I passed in the "sap-iapp-state". Is there any other way to achieve the possibility.

Please help if there is any other way.

entry.title: S4HANA Cloud - Fiori Quick Deploy - Change the tile title
entry.content:
Hello dear SAP community,

I am facing a quite funny issue. I have deployed a Fiori RAP application on the stack of a S4HANA Cloud system (Public) via the Quick Deploy function in ADT. After while the key user decided, the Fiori tile is not correct and want me to change it.

Unfortunately (from some mysterious reason) the Launchpad App Descriptor object (which contains the needed data) is not editable in Eclipse ADT, when I try to change the field Title in the Tile Details I get this error:

I tried also use the Maintain Translation app to translate it to second language and then from the second language to the source language, but the source language texts cannot be changed. 

Does anyone have an idea how to proceed? Last solution for me is to create a new project in BAS and deploy it again from BAS with the correct title, but that's too complicated for the fact I want to change just the Fiori tile text. I also cannot redeploy it from ADT, it seems the Quick Deploy can be executed only once per service binding.

 

Thank you in advance,

Matus

entry.title: SAP Secure Login Client within Learning the Basics of SAP Fiori learning module
entry.content:
I am currently doing the  Learning the Basics of SAP Fiori  module learning path, module Using SAP Fiory Launchpad. And I am stuck at the first practical exercise Task 1: Prepare the SAP Learning System. According to the instructions, I would need to go to a page https://learning.sap.com and log-in into SAP Secure Login Client. I expected to find something similar to hand-on practice system, but I did not manage to find anything similar. From what I could google, I found out that SAP Secure Login Client is a separate software and there seem to be no free access to it. Probably, I am doing something wrong, and I would be really thankful for some clarifications.

Regards, Anastasia

entry.title: User default values from Fiori Launchpad
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

How we can retrieve user's default values from fiori launchpad in a custom UI5 application?
I checked in sap ushell services but could not find any option.
Please suggest.


Thank you!

entry.title: Filter Value help results in Child entity based on parent entity field value in CAP JAVA
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have a CAP JAVA based oData service, which is composed on a parent and its child entities. Now, we have a requirement to add a Value Help for one of the child entity field. I have a issue here I want to filter records in this Value help on the basis of a field value from its parent entity.

Could you please help?

Thanks,
Shivani

entry.title: Failed to resolve navigation target "#Bank-manageExtended"
entry.content:
Hi all,

I extended the Manage Banks App F1574 using an adaptation project, and when I open the app, it displays the following error message:

Failed to resolve navigation target "#Bank-manageExtended". This is most likely caused by an incorrect SAP Fiori launchpad content configuration or by a missing role assignment.

Here is the target mapping and the tile configuration:

 

The content of the manifest.appdescr_variant

{
  "fileName": "manifest",
  "layer": "CUSTOMER_BASE",
  "fileType": "appdescr_variant",
  "reference": "fin.cash.bankmaster.manage",
  "id": "customer.customer.fin.cash.bankmaster.manage.extension",
  "namespace": "apps/fin.cash.bankmaster.manage/appVariants/customer.customer.fin.cash.bankmaster.manage.extension/",
  "content": [
    {
      "changeType": "appdescr_app_setTitle",
      "content": {},
      "texts": {
        "i18n": "i18n/i18n.properties"
      }
    },
    {
      "changeType": "appdescr_ui5_addNewModelEnhanceWith",
      "content": {
        "modelId": "i18n",
        "bundleUrl": "i18n/i18n.properties",
        "supportedLocales": [
          ""
        ],
        "fallbackLocale": ""
      }
    },
    {
      "changeType": "appdescr_ui5_addNewModelEnhanceWith",
      "content": {
        "modelId": "@i18n",
        "bundleUrl": "i18n/i18n.properties",
        "supportedLocales": [
          ""
        ],
        "fallbackLocale": ""
      }
    },
    {
      "changeType": "appdescr_ui5_setMinUI5Version",
      "content": {
        "minUI5Version": "1.108.23"
      }
    }
  ]
}

and /sap/bc/ui2/app_index/ui5_app_info?id=customer.customer.fin.cash.bankmaster.manage.extension

{
  "customer.customer.fin.cash.bankmaster.manage.extension": {
    "dependencies": [
      {
        "id": "customer.customer.fin.cash.bankmaster.manage.extension",
        "componentId": "fin.cash.bankmaster.manage",
        "type": "UI5COMP",
        "url": "/sap/bc/ui5_ui5/sap/fin_bank",
        "descriptorUrl": "/sap/bc/lrep/content/~20251209123247.9324920~/apps/fin.cash.bankmaster.manage/appVariants/customer.customer.fin.cash.bankmaster.manage.extension/manifest.appdescr_variant",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "sap.ui.table",
        "componentId": "sap.ui.table",
        "type": "UI5LIB",
        "url": "",
        "descriptorUrl": "",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "sap.suite.ui.commons",
        "componentId": "sap.suite.ui.commons",
        "type": "UI5LIB",
        "url": "",
        "descriptorUrl": "",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "sap.fe.placeholder",
        "componentId": "sap.fe.placeholder",
        "type": "UI5LIB",
        "url": "",
        "descriptorUrl": "",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "sap.ui.core",
        "componentId": "sap.ui.core",
        "type": "UI5LIB",
        "url": "",
        "descriptorUrl": "",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "sap.ui.generic.app",
        "componentId": "sap.ui.generic.app",
        "type": "UI5LIB",
        "url": "",
        "descriptorUrl": "",

        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {

      {

      {

      },
      {
        "id": "/sap/opu/odata/sap/YBMD_FCLM_BM_SRV/",

      {
        "id": "sap.f",
        "componentId": "sap.f",
        "type": "UI5LIB",
        "url": "",
        "descriptorUrl": "",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {

        "componentId": "sap.suite.ui.generic.template",
        "type": "UI5LIB",
        "url": "",

        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "sap.fin.central.lib",
        "componentId": "sap.fin.central.lib",
        "type": "UI5LIB",

        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "sap.se.mi.plm.lib.attachmentservice",
        "componentId": "sap.se.mi.plm.lib.attachmentservice",
        "type": "UI5LIB",
        "url": "/sap/bc/ui5_ui5/sap/plm_ath_cres1",
        "descriptorUrl": "/sap/bc/lrep/content/~20251209123311.4398020~/apps/sap.se.mi.plm.lib.attachmentservice/app/sap/plm_ath_cres1/manifest.appdescr",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "/sap/opu/odata/sap/CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV/",
        "componentId": "",
        "type": "ODATA",
        "typeVersion": "2.0",
        "url": "",
        "descriptorUrl": "",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "sap.s4h.cfnd.featuretoggle",
        "componentId": "sap.s4h.cfnd.featuretoggle",
        "type": "UI5LIB",
        "url": "/sap/bc/ui5_ui5/sap/featuretoggles1",
        "descriptorUrl": "/sap/bc/lrep/content/~20251209123229.0314360~/apps/sap.s4h.cfnd.featuretoggle/app/sap/featuretoggles1/manifest.appdescr",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "sap.collaboration",
        "componentId": "sap.collaboration",
        "type": "UI5LIB",
        "url": "",
        "descriptorUrl": "",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "sap.ushell",
        "componentId": "sap.ushell",
        "type": "UI5LIB",
        "url": "",
        "descriptorUrl": "",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "/sap/opu/odata/SAP/CA_FM_FEATURE_TOGGLE_STATUS_SRV",
        "componentId": "",
        "type": "ODATA",
        "typeVersion": "2.0",
        "url": "",
        "descriptorUrl": "",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "customer.customer.fin.cash.bankmaster.manage.extension",
        "componentId": "",
        "type": "APPVARID",
        "url": "/sap/bc/lrep/flex/data/~a9jzRjGlwBHbsAUULWgdfQ+2JAU=~/customer.customer.fin.cash.bankmaster.manage.extension",
        "descriptorUrl": "",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "sap.ui.fl",
        "componentId": "sap.ui.fl",
        "type": "UI5LIB",
        "url": "",
        "descriptorUrl": "",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      }
    ],
    "error": false,
    "noDescriptor": false
  }
}

What am I doing wrong?

Best regards

entry.title: Reload an Object Page with a different record after a custom action
entry.content:
Greetings, community experts.

I am working on a SAP CAP app (NodeJS) with SAP Fiori Elements on SAP BTP. At the SAP Fiori Elements level I am also using some Extension.

What I want to do is simple to explain: After executing certain custom action on an Object Page, I need to reload that page, but with changing the key, to display another object, i.e. another record of the same entity... For example, the Object Page is displaying Batch 10001, and after the custom action, the object page should reload and display Batch 10015.

How could I do this?

Thanks in advance for any help and/or suggestions.

entry.title: Fiori Elements OData V4 draft create navigates to transient $uid path instead of server draft path,
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I am facing an issue in a CAP + Fiori Elements OData V4 draft application

Problem:

 When I press Create in one specific config app (AggrConfigService / Aggregator), the draft creation flow breaks.

The UI navigates/binds to a transient client-side path like: AggrConfigService.Aggregator($uid=id-1773199858692-511)

CAP then returns: 404 Invalid resource path "AggrConfigService.Aggregator($uid=...)" What is strange is that the POST itself succeeds first.

Observed request/response

1) Create request: POST Aggregator Body: {"AggregatorID":"Test"}

Response: HTTP/1.1 204 No Content location: Aggregator.drafts('Test') 2) Immediately after that, UI5 sends: GET Aggregator($uid=id-1773199858692-511)?$select=AggregatorID,AggregatorName,HasActiveEntity,HasDraftEntity,IsActive,IsActiveEntity&$expand=DraftAdministrativeData($select=DraftIsCreatedByMe,DraftUUID,InProcessByUser) Response: HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found {"error":{"code":"404","message":"Invalid resource path \"AggrConfigService.Aggregator($uid=id-1773199858692-511)\""}}

So the backend seems to create the draft successfully and returns a draft location, but the frontend still tries to read via the transient $uid path instead of switching to the canonical draft path.

Environment:

SAP CAP Node.js - @sap/cds 8.9.8 - SAPUI5 1.142.0 - Fiori Elements OData V4 - Draft-enabled service - Cloud Foundry deployment on BTP

Questions:

1) Has anyone seen Fiori Elements OData V4 draft create navigate to a transient `$uid` path like this after a successful POST?

2) Is there any known issue/pattern where FE object-page routing or binding keeps the transient context instead of resolving to the canonical draft location returned by CAP?

Thanks a lot.

Best Regards,

Muthu

SAPUI5 SAP Fiori Elements SAP Cloud Application Programming Model  OData SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime and environment

entry.title: Standard date format issue in Smart Business KPI report
entry.content:
Hi All,

we're facing an issue with Date-Display for CDS-Based Queries in Smart Business KPIs. The date values are not displayed according to user settings (for example dd.mm.yyyy). Instead, the KPI report shows dates in internal format yyyymmdd. Standard format is not working dd.mm.yyyy (yyyymmdd) .

  • In the KPI report (Manage KPIs and Reports), all date fields display as yyyymmdd.
  • The expected behavior is that dates should follow the user’s regional/date settings (e.g., dd.mm.yyyy(yyyymmdd)).

  • In the OData service response, the date dimension contains two fields:

    • PlannedGoodsIssueDate
    • PlannedGoodsIssueDate_T

  • We are not explicitly defining any _T fields in our CDS views, but the _T field still appears in OData.

  • The OData service is using standard generic analytics runtime classes:

    • MPC: CL_NAT_GENERIC_MPC
    • DPC: CL_NAT_GENERIC_DPC

  • When I inspect the Fiori KPI app network calls, the GET request uses $select, but it does not include:

    • PlannedGoodsIssueDate_T

    So the KPI report only fetches PlannedGoodsIssueDate and the _T field never reaches the UI.

 

  1. What is the recommended way to ensure date fields are displayed in the user’s date format in KPI tiles?
  2. Is there any known configuration / best practice / SAP note to address this issue?

Any guidance would be appreciated.

Thanks in advance,

PA

entry.title: oModel.read is not working - OData V2
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am new on the SAP BTP/BAS world and I am trying to read a oData model v2 but it is not working.

return Controller.extend("XXX", {
    onInit: async function () { 
      try {
        await this._PickedMaterials(); 
      } catch (e) {
        console.error(e);
        MessageBox.error("Not able to select picket items.");
      }
    },

And here is my funcion _Picked Material

  _PickedMaterials: async function () {
debugger;
var oModel = this.getOwnerComponent().getModel("C_EWM_ODOOVPGQ_2_CDS");
oModel.read("/C_EWM_ODOOVPGQ_2", {
        urlParameters: { "$top": 20 },
        success: function (data) {
          console.log("SUCCESS DATA:", data);
          console.log("RESULTS:", data.results);
          debugger; 
        },
        error: function (err) {
          console.error("READ ERROR:", err);
          debugger; 
        }
      });

The code reaches the first debugger in the function but it does not enter the sucess or the error conditions. 


oModel is correct:

 and on debug mode shows like this:


Then it gets to oModel.read("/C_EWM_ODOOVPGQ_2", { It just jumps straight away

I have some console.logs in place like 

oModel.setUseBatch(false);

oModel.attachRequestSent((e) => {
        console.log("RequestSent:", e.getParameters()); // url, method, async, etc.
      });

C_EWM_ODOOVPGQ_2 is maintained in the backend and response is 200
I can also see the Live data for C_EWM_ODOOVPGQ_2_CDS in Service Center in BAS
OData was added using Service Manager
There is a API being used and working in this app

Any idea what is wrong here?

Thanks
Andréa

entry.title: Best IDE for Fiori Development with NetWeaver 7.52 SP4 (MiniSAP)
entry.content:
Hello,
I am looking for recommendations on the most suitable IDE for developing Fiori applications with NetWeaver 7.52 SP4 (MiniSAP). I initially attempted to use Visual Studio Code, but encountered issues when trying to connect the MiniSAP system to the IDE. Specifically, VS Code does not seem to detect OData v4 services from MiniSAP.
My assumption is that MiniSAP may have limitations regarding OData v4 support. Given this, I would like to know if there are alternative IDEs that offer capabilities comparable to VS Code for Fiori application development.
Thank you in advance for your insights.
Best regards,
Dariel Alves.
